title: Text Normalization
concept: Text normalization cleans raw text by lowercasing, removing punctuation, and stripping unwanted characters to reduce vocabulary size and noise.

exercises.prompt: What is the main problem with raw text before we clean it?

options.text: It is messy, inconsistent, and contains extra punctuation or spaces.
It is always perfectly clean and ready for computers.
It only contains numbers and no words at all.

exercises.explain: Capital letters, punctuation, and extra spaces make raw text messy and inconsistent for computers to read.

cards.title: Messy Text, Clean Mind
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how computers understand what we write, even when we use weird capitalization, random punctuation, or extra spaces? To a computer, 'Hello!', 'hello', and 'HELLO' look like three totally different words. Let's find out how we clean up this text mess!

cards.title: What is Text Normalization?
cards.body: Text normalization is the process of cleaning raw text to make it uniform. By converting everything to lowercase, removing punctuation, and stripping away extra spaces, we help AI models focus on the meaning of the words instead of getting distracted by minor spelling or formatting differences.

cards.title: The Sock Drawer Organizer
cards.body: Imagine you are sorting a big box of socks. Some are crumpled up, some are inside out, and others are folded. If you flatten and pair them up nicely first, it is much easier to count them. Text normalization is like smoothing out all the crumpled socks so they look identical and are easy to organize.

cards.title: Normalization in Action
cards.body: Before normalization, an AI might see 'Run!', 'run', and 'RUN' as three completely different words. After converting them to lowercase and removing punctuation, all three become simply 'run'. This reduces the vocabulary size the AI has to learn and helps it recognize patterns much faster!

cards.title: Myth: Normalization Ruins Text
cards.body: Many people think text normalization strips away the actual meaning of the sentences. In reality, it only removes the 'noise'—like messy formatting and punctuation—so that the computer can understand the core message much more clearly.

cards.title: Before and After Normalization
cards.body: See how normalization transforms messy, inconsistent inputs into clean, uniform data.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use text normalization before training an AI model?

options.text: It makes the text longer and more complex.
It reduces vocabulary size and removes unwanted noise.
It adds fun emojis to the text automatically.
It translates the text into different languages.

exercises.explain: Normalizing text reduces the number of unique words (vocabulary size) and removes extra formatting noise, making it much easier for AI models to learn.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Text normalization keeps all exclamation points and question marks exactly as they are.
exercises.explain: Punctuation marks like commas, periods, and exclamation points are usually removed during normalization to simplify the text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how we normalize text capitalization.
exercises.sentence: We convert all letters to [blank] so the computer doesn't get confused by capital letters.
exercises.explain: Changing letters to lowercase (like turning 'DOG' into 'dog') ensures that the computer treats them as the exact same word.

exercises.prompt: An AI is trained to output normalized text. Which of these outputs is correctly normalized?
exercises.explain: Option B is the only output where all letters are lowercase and all punctuation (like the exclamation mark) has been removed.

exercises.prompt: If you apply text normalization to the tweet '#Python is FUN!!!', which clean text will you get?
exercises.explain: To normalize this tweet, you lowercase all the letters, remove the punctuation (like exclamation marks and hashtags), and strip extra spaces. This turns '#Python is FUN!!!' into 'python is fun'.
